title: DIY Entryway Table
summary: This DIY entryway table has a modern design and can be customized to whatever color and leg style you choose.
description: This DIY entryway table has a modern design and can be customized to whatever color and leg style you choose. (You could also add an optional drawer if you choose to modify the design.)

steps.title: Cut Your Wood
steps.body: Use your 4 1/2" circular saw (I clamped a speed square and a board down to use as a guide) to cut your wood to the specified dimensions.

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Drill pocket holes as follows: 3 at each end of your 46 1/2" long center shelf piece; 3 along one side of each of your 1x6 divider pieces; 3 at each end of your 12" long side pieces.

steps.title: Sand Smooth
steps.body: Sand everything smooth using a random orbit sander.

steps.title: Assemble Sides and Dividers
steps.body: Working with the table upside down for now, attach the sides to one of your 48" long project panels using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws. Add your 1x6 divider pieces (I positioned mine 18" apart), also using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Add Center Shelf
steps.body: Attach your 46 1/2" project panel (center shelf) to the sides using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws, then use 1 1/4" wood screws to attach it to the 1x6 boards as well.

steps.title: Add Bottom Panel
steps.body: Add your bottom panel (remember we are currently working upside down and will flip it over), attaching it with 1 1/4" wood screws.

steps.title: Prime and Paint
steps.body: Flip over your table base and prime and paint (or stain) it. This one is painted a mountain olive green.

steps.title: Attach Legs
steps.body: Flip it back over and attach your prefabricated legs (these came from Amazon) using 1/2" wood screws.
